checks.finding: The round-2 Major (AC-008 changed-files exclusion scope) is verifiably fixed at all three cited locations: requirements.md:142-147 (REQ-002 seed description: 'the case-(a) changed-files input excludes both'), requirements.md:454-460 (Edge Cases: 'BOTH...marker-bearing files...must never appear'), and acceptance-tests.md:17 (AC-008 row: 'excluding BOTH marker-bearing files'). The Edge Cases bullet self-disambiguates via its own next clause and is anchored by the unambiguous REQ-002 scoping to 'case-(a)' only and by AC-008/AC-009's independently unambiguous table rows, so no residual two-implementer fork remains. No other terms/states introduce a new fork: Field Definitions are concrete, and AC-010's 'at least one review loop' is pinned to 'spec-review round 1' by the AC-010 table row.

No direct conflict found between goals, ACs, constraints, and non-goals. The seed's fixture 'tasks.md' is explicitly distinguished from the Non-goals' Phase-2 tasks.md by its own Field Definition text ('inert fixture data...never driven through a review loop'). AC-013/AC-016's 'zero edits' self-healing claim does not conflict with T-002's own edit to the same file, since the zero-edits claim is scoped to each task's own deliverable (INV-033/034). Security Boundaries B4, gh-non-invocation Field Definition, and AC-004/Edge Cases Category guidance are mutually consistent.

Material negative/boundary paths are enumerated with explicit red-capable pairs: AC-002/TEST-002 (HITL reproduces-on-iteration-3 negative branch and wiring canary), AC-003/TEST-003 (threshold-mutation negative self-check), AC-006/TEST-006 (threshold-0 self-check), AC-008/AC-009 (positive/negative check-placeholders pair). AC-013/AC-016 rely on an externally observable SKIP-to-green transition with an explicit, reasoned justification in acceptance-tests.md Notes ('the named SKIP that already exists at HEAD IS the red side'). AC-005 is scoped by an explicit non-goal to the attempt<3 direction with the reason cited.

All four Assumptions are investigation-cited stable facts or carry an explicit forward-looking contingency with a named consequence and out-of-scope declaration. All seven Open Questions (OQ-1..OQ-7) are marked RESOLVED with concrete resolutions cross-referenced to Field Definitions, AC rows, and investigation.md, leaving no unresolved decision that would block design/task decomposition.

The spec implies a governance/irreversible-action boundary (wfi-audit-cycle's Human-Blocked convergence guard and the gh issue create action behind it) and makes it testable: AC-003 locks the one-directional Audit-Attempt>=3 -> Human-Blocked rule across 0->1->2->3 with a threshold-mutation negative self-check; AC-004 makes gh-issue-create categorically unreachable by construction plus a grep self-check; AC-005 cross-validates against two real governance documents with a SHA-256 immutability assertion. Sufficient observable proof; SKIP does not apply since the boundary is in scope.

The specification supplies concrete, resolved detail sufficient to hand off without invented product behavior: Field Definitions pin the cross-cutting terms; Edge Cases enumerate CI-resilience constraints each tied to an investigation citation; Security Boundaries and Risks/Mitigations are explicit; every AC maps 1:1 to a REQ and cites concrete pass/fail behavior. Design-phase items are appropriately referenced but not required by this spec, consistent with the calibration's Phase 1/Phase 2 boundary.
